seo.page_title: BYD Atto 3 2023 import to NZ from Japan
seo.meta_description: Import a 2023 BYD Atto 3 EV from Japan to NZ. 60kWh battery, 204hp motor, low running costs. NZ-based team handles auctions, shipping and entry steps.
seo.h1_tag: Import a 2023 BYD Atto 3 to NZ
seo.og_title: BYD Atto 3 2023 EV – Import from Japan to NZ
seo.og_description: Looking at a 2023 BYD Atto 3? We source from Japanese auctions, outline real landed costs, and help you bring one into NZ with a clear, step-by-step process.

blocks.hero_h1: Import a 2023 BYD Atto 3 to NZ
blocks.hero_subtitle: Bring in a 2023 BYD Atto 3 from Japan with a 60kWh battery, 204hp motor and 310Nm of torque — set up as a good honest daily EV for NZ roads.
proof_points: Transparent pricing — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: Kiwis like the 2023 BYD Atto 3 for its 60kWh battery and roughly 420 km of official range, which keeps day-to-day charging simple. The 204hp front motor and 310Nm make it quick off the line for city gaps and motorway on-ramps, while DC charging up to 220kW means shorter stops on longer trips. It works well as a reliable daily driver with low running costs.

savings_block.headline: What you could save vs buying a BYD Atto 3 on the yard
savings_block.body: Recent 2023 BYD Atto 3s at NZ dealers often sit above $60,000, depending on trim and mileage. Importing from Japan, we typically see landed costs in the $45,000–$60,000 range once you factor in auction price, shipping, NZ entry certification and on-road costs. The exact number depends on the car you choose, but we’ll show you the breakdown before you commit.

cta_form.headline: Find your BYD Atto 3 — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Here’s a 2023 BYD Atto 3 on charge, showing the 60kWh battery status and range estimate on the central display so you can see how much is left at a glance.
blocks.main_usp_deep: With a 60kWh battery and an estimated 420 km official range, the 2023 BYD Atto 3 covers most NZ commutes and weekend trips without constant top-ups. The 204hp front motor and 310Nm of torque give it easy pace for overtakes and hills, helped by strong regenerative braking in stop–start traffic. DC charging up to 220kW lets you add useful range quickly on the road, while home charging keeps running costs predictable. For many buyers it’s a straightforward way into a modern EV without going overboard on size or complexity.

buyer_persona.headline: Is this car right for you?
buyer_persona.body: You want a reliable daily runabout that’s cheap to run, with enough range for commuting, school runs and the odd trip out of town. You’re not chasing badges, you just want a modern EV that’s easy to live with and charges quickly when you need it. If that sounds like you, the 2023 BYD Atto 3 is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We’ve been helping Kiwis bring in cars from Japan since 2009, with more than 1,000 vehicles sourced across EVs, hybrids and everyday runabouts. You get full auction documentation, our notes on condition and value, and a clear view of costs from Japan to your driveway. We’re an NZ-based team you can call or email, and we work to find cars that typically meet NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: Gives enough capacity for most NZ commutes and weekend trips without daily charging.
Lets you cover longer runs between main centres with fewer charging stops if you plan ahead.
Plenty of shove for motorway on-ramps, hills and safe overtakes, even with the family on board.
Instant pull from a standstill, making city driving and gaps in traffic easier to manage.
Shorter top-ups on compatible fast chargers, handy for road trips or busy days.

cta_mid_page.headline: Keen on an Atto 3? Let’s price one up
cta_mid_page.subtext: Tell us the year, colour and budget you’re aiming for and we’ll come back with real auction examples and estimated landed costs.
cta_mid_page.button_text: Happy to run the numbers for you.

models_comparison.intro: Here’s how different 2023 BYD Atto 3 options from Japan can vary when you’re choosing what to import.

rows.notes: For a 2023 BYD Atto 3 you’re within the latest safety and tech updates, and it typically meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted.
Japanese-market Atto 3s commonly use the 60kWh battery with a 204hp front motor and 310Nm, giving an estimated 420 km official range. Some trims may differ in equipment rather than battery size.
Higher trims may add extra driver aids, upgraded interior materials and more comfort features. Lower trims can be better value if you’re focused on range and running costs over extras.

As a late-model EV, the Atto 3 typically meets NZ entry standards in most cases if documentation is clean. You’ll still need to allow for entry certification, any CO2 charges that apply at the time, and standard EV safety checks.
2023 BYD Atto 3s are appearing more often in Japanese auctions, but numbers still vary week to week. Being flexible on colour and trim can shorten the wait and sharpen pricing.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others. For EVs like the BYD Atto 3, we pay close attention to battery-related notes and range comments on each shortlisted car.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common trims or colours, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a 2023 BYD Atto 3 from Japan is straightforward, but it helps to understand the common risks and how we handle them.

items.risk: Overpaying for a used BYD Atto 3 compared with local options.
items.solution: We track recent auction results and NZ retail pricing so you can see how your shortlisted car compares. You get a full landed-cost estimate before you decide to bid.

items.risk: Hidden costs between the hammer price and getting the car on your driveway.
items.solution: We outline the main cost buckets up front: auction price, our fee, shipping and insurance, NZ entry certification, port charges, and on-road costs. That way you can budget properly.

items.risk: Uncertainty about condition and auction grades on a late-model EV.
items.solution: You receive the original auction sheet, photos and our comments on grade, visible wear and any notes about the battery or electronics. We look for cars that typically meet NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: You tell us the sort of BYD Atto 3 you’re after, your budget and how you’ll use it. We scan the Japanese auctions, send you real cars with full documentation and talk through the pros and cons. If you want to go ahead, we handle the bidding, shipping and NZ entry steps, and you decide how and when you’d like to take delivery.

faq_block.q: Is a 60kWh BYD Atto 3 enough battery for daily NZ driving?
faq_block.a: For most people, yes. The estimated 420 km official range comfortably covers typical commuting, school runs and errands, with plenty left over. Even allowing for real-world conditions, many owners only need to charge a few times a week.

faq_block.q: How much does it cost to import a 2023 BYD Atto 3 from Japan?
faq_block.a: Most 2023 Atto 3s we see land between about $45,000 and $60,000, depending on auction price, mileage and trim. On top of the hammer price you’ll need to allow for our fee, shipping and insurance, NZ entry certification, port charges and on-road costs.

faq_block.q: Will a Japanese BYD Atto 3 meet NZ compliance rules?
faq_block.a: A 2023 BYD Atto 3 typically meets NZ entry standards in most cases if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted, but final sign-off only happens after physical inspection here. We avoid cars with obvious red flags on the auction sheet to improve the odds of a smooth process.

faq_block.q: How long does it take from winning an auction to driving the car in NZ?
faq_block.a: Once the car is bought, shipping to NZ is usually around 2–4 weeks depending on sailings, then you need to allow time for port processing, entry certification and registration. In total, many buyers see 6–10 weeks from winning the auction to being on the road.

faq_block.q: How do auction grades relate to the condition of a BYD Atto 3?
faq_block.a: Auction grades give a shorthand view of body and interior condition, but the notes and photos matter just as much. We translate the sheet, highlight any issues and only recommend cars where the grade, mileage and condition line up sensibly for the price.

footer_cta.headline: Thinking about a 2023 BYD Atto 3 from Japan?
footer_cta.subtext: Tell us what you’re after and we’ll come back with real auction options, estimated landed costs and our honest view on value so you can decide in your own time.
footer_cta.button_text: Happy to run the numbers for you.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get updates on BYD Atto 3 listings, recent auction prices and simple guides to importing EVs to NZ.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based import service that’s been sourcing vehicles from Japan since 2009. We focus on good honest cars, from EVs like the BYD Atto 3 through to everyday family runabouts. Our role is to connect you with the Japanese auctions, explain the risks and costs, and manage the process through to NZ entry certification.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: When we talk about landed cost for a 2023 BYD Atto 3, we mean the main expenses to get the car from a Japanese auction to registered on NZ roads.

rows.notes: The hammer price you pay for the car in Japan, plus local auction fees and our service fee.
Transport from the auction to port, sea freight to NZ and marine insurance while it’s on the water.
Port charges, entry certification, any work needed to meet NZ standards, registration and plates, plus any CO2 or other government charges that apply at the time.

auction_checklist.check: Battery and range comments on the auction sheet
auction_checklist.why: Notes about reduced range or battery warnings can signal harder use or potential future costs.
Moderate, consistent mileage is usually better for an EV battery than very low use or long storage periods.
Higher grades often mean fewer repairs and less wear, which helps with both resale and day-to-day enjoyment.

auction_checklist.check: Any mention of modifications or aftermarket electronics
auction_checklist.why: Non-standard wiring or parts can complicate NZ entry certification and future servicing.

auction_checklist.check: Service records or dealer stamps where available
auction_checklist.why: Evidence of regular servicing suggests the car has been looked after, even on a late-model EV.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: You need a seven-seater or large cargo space for regular work use.
You often tow heavy loads where a larger vehicle or different powertrain would be more suitable.
You’re not comfortable with the idea of importing and would rather pay extra for a car you can see on a local yard today.

alternatives_block.intro: If the BYD Atto 3 doesn’t quite line up with how you drive, there are other EVs worth a look.

rows.better_if: You mainly do city and short inter-city trips and want a slightly smaller hatchback.
rows.tradeoff: Less cabin space than the Atto 3 and different charging hardware, but often sharper pricing.

battery_health_block.headline: Battery health on a 2023 BYD Atto 3
battery_health_block.body: On a late-model EV like the 2023 BYD Atto 3, outright battery failure is uncommon, but how the car has been used still matters. We look for signs of sensible charging habits and normal daily use so you’re not buying someone else’s experiment. Where data is available, we compare indicated range and usage to what you’d expect from a 60kWh pack.
checklist: Look for consistent mileage that suggests regular commuting rather than long storage
Check for any warning lights or battery-related notes on the auction sheet
Compare the displayed range at a given charge level with what’s typical for a 60kWh Atto 3
Avoid cars with signs of heavy commercial or ride-share use where possible

charging_block.headline: Charging a BYD Atto 3 in NZ
charging_block.home_charging: Most owners will charge a 2023 BYD Atto 3 at home overnight using a wall-mounted charger or upgraded socket. With a 60kWh battery, an overnight charge is usually enough to go from low to full, and many people just top up a bit each night.
charging_block.public_charging: On the road, the Atto 3 supports DC rapid charging up to 220kW on compatible chargers, so you can add a useful chunk of range in a short stop. Slower AC public chargers are handy for top-ups while you’re at work or shopping.
caveats: Fast charging is handy, but relying on it every day can be harder on any EV battery over time.
Public charger availability and pricing vary by region, so it’s worth checking the networks you’ll use most.
If you live in an apartment or shared parking, plan where and how you’ll charge before committing.

ev_running_costs_block.headline: Running costs: BYD Atto 3 vs a non-EV daily
ev_running_costs_block.body: With a 60kWh battery and home charging, most owners see much lower day-to-day energy costs than a similar-sized non-EV SUV. Servicing is generally simpler too, with no oil changes and fewer moving parts. You’ll still need to budget for tyres, brakes, WOFs and any software or cabin filter checks, but overall it’s usually a cheap-to-run daily.
items: Home charging often works out to a fraction of the cost per km of non-EV, depending on your power plan.
Regenerative braking reduces wear on brake pads in stop–start city driving.
Fewer fluids and filters to replace compared with a traditional vehicle, which can mean less time in the workshop.

range_real_world_block.headline: Real-world range from the 60kWh battery
range_real_world_block.body: The 2023 BYD Atto 3 has an estimated 420 km official range, but what you see day to day will depend on speed, weather and how you drive. Around town with plenty of regenerative braking, many drivers will get close to the official figure. On the open road at 100 km/h, especially in winter, it’s sensible to plan for less and build in a buffer for hills and headwinds.
items: City use with lots of slowing and stopping usually gives the best range thanks to regenerative braking.
High-speed motorway runs and strong winds can trim range, so plan charging stops on longer trips.
Using heating or air conditioning heavily will also eat into range, particularly in colder months.

model_deep_dive_block.headline: What stands out about the 2023 BYD Atto 3
model_deep_dive_block.body: The 2023 BYD Atto 3 combines a 60kWh battery with a 204hp front motor and 310Nm of torque, giving it easy performance for everyday NZ driving. Regenerative braking helps in stop–start traffic in cities like Auckland and Wellington, while the stable range suits flatter regions such as Christchurch and Hamilton. DC charging up to 220kW means it’s practical for longer trips if you’re happy to plan your stops around the public network.
items: Comfortable size for city parking while still offering family-friendly space.
Modern EV tech without going to a very large or very expensive battery pack.
Good match for mixed use: commuting, school runs and weekend drives.

trim_comparison_block.headline: Choosing the right BYD Atto 3 from Japan
trim_comparison_block.body: Japanese-market 2023 BYD Atto 3s share the same basic 60kWh battery and 204hp motor, but trim levels can change the feel of the car. Higher trims may add more driver assistance, upgraded audio and extra comfort touches, while simpler trims can be better value if you’re focused on range and running costs.
items: Entry trims: usually cloth interiors and simpler audio, but the same core battery and motor.
Mid trims: often add more safety tech and convenience features that make daily use easier.
Top trims: can include extra comfort and cosmetic upgrades that may or may not be worth the extra cost to you.
